product-no-team.title: You have a product in your head and no team to build it.
product-no-team.problem: You can see the thing clearly. You just have no developers, designers, or budget to assemble them.
product-no-team.approach: You describe what you want to exist and make the calls on what it should be. The interface, the logic, and the working thing get built with you.
product-no-team.ownership: You own all of it, yours to change.

business-unknown-field.title: You want to open a business in a field you don't know yet.
business-unknown-field.problem: You have the idea but you'd need a strategist, an analyst, and a row of consultants just to begin.
business-unknown-field.approach: You bring the idea. You get the market read, the model, the brand, the plan, and the first steps, in language you understand and shaped to your situation.
business-unknown-field.shape: A clear plan in days; the build that follows scoped with you.
business-unknown-field.ownership: Yours to decide and move on.

campaign-at-scale.title: You need a campaign across several platforms at once.
campaign-at-scale.problem: Different formats, different languages, and one of you. Stitching it from five tools makes it incoherent.
campaign-at-scale.approach: You set the intent and the message. You get the concept, the assets, and the variations across formats and languages, coherent because it was orchestrated as one.
campaign-at-scale.shape: A full campaign in a fraction of the usual time.

manual-process.title: Your team is drowning in a manual process.
manual-process.problem: The work is repetitive and slow, and every fix needs a vendor or a new hire.
manual-process.approach: You describe the workflow and where it hurts. You get it mapped, redesigned, and automated, with you deciding what stays in human hands.

raise-story-numbers.title: You're raising and need the story and the numbers.
raise-story-numbers.problem: You need a model, a thesis, and a deck that hold up under scrutiny, fast.
raise-story-numbers.approach: You bring the business. You get the model, the thesis, and the deck, built to hold up, in your voice, with you directing what it argues.

internal-tool.title: You want a tool your team uses every day.
internal-tool.problem: A CRM or internal system shaped to how you actually work, not a generic product you bend yourself around.
internal-tool.approach: You describe how your team works. You get a system built to fit.

new-market.title: You're entering a new market.
new-market.problem: You need research, localization, and a go-to-market built for that market specifically.
new-market.approach: You bring the ambition. You get the research, the localization, and the go-to-market, built for that market, with you steering.
new-market.shape: A real plan and the assets to start, quickly.

idea-carried-for-years.title: You've carried an idea for years and never built it.
idea-carried-for-years.problem: It never happened because it always needed people you couldn't pull together.
idea-carried-for-years.approach: You finally describe it. The intelligence helps you shape it and bring it into existence, with you as the author the whole way.
idea-carried-for-years.shape: From idea to something real, at last.

build-a-product-or-app.name: Build a product or app
build-a-product-or-app.headline: Turn the product in your head into a real one, without assembling a team first.
build-a-product-or-app.lead: You describe what you want to exist. You make the calls on what it should be. Stryvia builds it with you, and you own it.
problems: I have a product idea and no development team.
I've paid for builds before and ended up with something I don't control.
I need a working version fast, not a six-month roadmap.

sub.label: FROM IDEA TO SPEC
sub.text: You describe it in plain words; it becomes a clear, buildable shape you approve before anything is made.

sub.label: THE BUILD
sub.text: The interface, the logic, and the working thing get built with you directing the consequential calls.

sub.label: ITERATE AND OWN
sub.text: You change it whenever you want, because you own it — no lock-in, no dependency.

build-a-product-or-app.approach: It understands what the product is really for before proposing a shape, then orchestrates the design, the logic, and the build into one coherent thing instead of disconnected parts.

launch-a-new-venture.name: Launch a new venture
launch-a-new-venture.headline: Start the business without hiring a row of consultants first.
launch-a-new-venture.lead: You bring the idea. You get the market read, the model, the brand, and the first steps — shaped to your situation, in language you understand.
problems: I have an idea but I'd need a strategist and analysts just to begin.
I'm entering a field I don't fully understand yet.
I need to move before a competitor does, not wait on a team.

sub.label: THE MARKET READ
sub.text: Where the opportunity is, who it's for, and what it would take — specific to your situation, not a template.

sub.label: MODEL & PLAN
sub.text: The business model, the numbers, and the first concrete steps you can act on.

sub.label: BRAND & FIRST MOVES
sub.text: The identity and the assets to start, built coherently because one intelligence made them.

launch-a-new-venture.approach: It reads your situation like a senior advisor, brings market, model, and brand thinking together, and hands you a plan you can direct and own.

creative-and-content-production.name: Creative and content production
creative-and-content-production.headline: Produce at a scale a full production apparatus used to require.
creative-and-content-production.lead: You set the intent and the craft direction. You get the work — coherent across formats, made as one.
problems: I'm capped by headcount and budget, not by ideas.
Stitching content from five tools makes it incoherent.
I need volume and quality at once.

sub.label: CONCEPT
sub.text: The idea and the direction, shaped with you so it's yours, not a generic output.

sub.label: PRODUCTION AT SCALE
sub.text: The assets across formats and channels, produced as one coherent body of work.

sub.label: VARIATIONS
sub.text: Versions across languages and placements that stay on-brand because they came from one mind.

creative-and-content-production.approach: Orchestration is the point here: many models produce, and Stryvia binds their output into one coherent campaign-grade result.

marketing-and-growth.name: Marketing and growth
marketing-and-growth.headline: The growth capability you direct yourself, without an agency's cost and pace.
marketing-and-growth.lead: You set the message and the goal. You get campaigns, content, and the moves to grow — fast, and in your control.
problems: An agency is slow and expensive; an internal team is a big commitment.
I need campaigns across channels that actually cohere.
I want output and speed I control.

sub.label: CAMPAIGNS
sub.text: Concept to assets to variations, across formats and languages, orchestrated as one.

sub.label: CONTENT AT SCALE
sub.text: The volume you need, on-brand, without a content team to manage.

sub.label: GROWTH MOVES
sub.text: The next concrete steps to grow, scoped to where you actually are.

marketing-and-growth.approach: It understands your market and message first, then produces and orchestrates the work so the whole campaign is coherent rather than stitched.

operations-and-automation.name: Operations and automation
operations-and-automation.headline: Map, redesign, and automate the work that's eating your team's time.
operations-and-automation.lead: You describe the workflow and where it hurts. You decide what stays in human hands. The rest gets automated, and you own it.
problems: A manual process is slow and repetitive.
Every fix needs a vendor or a new hire.
I want an internal tool shaped to how we actually work.

sub.label: MAP THE WORKFLOW
sub.text: Where the work happens and where it hurts, made visible before anything changes.

sub.label: REDESIGN
sub.text: A better flow, with you deciding what stays human and what gets automated.

sub.label: AUTOMATE & OWN
sub.text: A working automation or internal tool, built to fit, owned and controlled by you.

operations-and-automation.approach: It understands your process deeply, then builds and automates the execution — not just advice, the working thing, with you in control.
